repo.name: helix-editor/helix
repo.scope: the Helix modal text editor (Rust, multi-crate Cargo workspace)

reference_claims.text: helix-core is a crate: the core editing primitives.
helix-view is a crate: UI abstractions / the imperative shell.
helix-term is a crate: the terminal UI (the main editor binary frontend).
helix-lsp is a crate: the Language Server Protocol client.
helix-dap is a crate: the Debug Adapter Protocol (DAP) client.
helix-event is a crate: primitives for defining and handling events within the editor.
helix-loader is a crate: functions for building, fetching, and loading external resources.
helix-tui is a crate: TUI primitives.
helix-stdx is a crate: extensions to the standard library.
helix-lsp-types is a crate: Language Server Protocol type definitions.
helix-core contains the basic building blocks used to construct the editor (core editing primitives).
helix-view is the imperative shell built on top of core that provides common editor logic; Document, View and Editor live here.
helix-term is the terminal frontend.
helix-lsp is a Language Server Protocol client.
helix-dap is a Debug Adapter Protocol client.
helix-loader provides functions for building, fetching, and loading external resources (e.g. grammars/config).
helix-view depends on helix-core (the view layer builds on top of core).
helix-term depends on helix-view (the terminal frontend builds on the view layer).
The layering is core -> view -> term: helix-core does not depend on helix-view or helix-term (core is the innermost functional layer).
Core primitives are functional: most operations do not modify data in place but return a new copy.
The main data structure for representing buffers is a Rope (the ropey library, re-exported); ropes are cheap to clone, enabling snapshots of text state.
A Selection is the multiple-selection primitive: it consists of Ranges, each with a moving head and an immovable anchor; a single cursor is a selection with one range whose head and anchor coincide.
Ropes are modified by constructing an OT-like Transaction that represents one coherent change, can be applied to the rope, and can be inverted to produce an undo.
A Document ties together the Rope, Selection(s), Syntax, History and language server into a representation of an open file.
Multiple Views can display the same Document, so the document stores selections per view (document.selection() takes a ViewId).
The Editor holds the global state: all open documents, a tree of view splits, the configuration, and a registry of language servers.
Syntax highlighting and AST interaction use tree-sitter (the Syntax type is the interface to tree-sitter ASTs).
helix-tui is forked from tui-rs (inspired by Cursive).
The Rope implementation used is the ropey library (re-exported by helix-core).
Core's design is heavily based on CodeMirror 6.
helix-term's main function sets up a new Application that runs the editor event loop.

emitted_claims.text: helix-core exists as a workspace member crate (the editor text-editing core library).
helix-core is the editor's text-editing core: owns the Rope buffer, selections, ChangeSet edit transactions, undo history, and tree-sitter syntax engine, with no terminal/UI concerns.
helix-view exists as a workspace member crate providing the editor-state model and UI abstractions for backends.
helix-view provides the editor-state model: the central Editor struct holding Document/View registries plus mode, registers, theme, diagnostics, and handler wiring.
helix-term exists as the application/binary crate producing the hx executable.
helix-term is the top-level orchestrator: run loop, Compositor UI layer stack, Terminal backend, Editor state, job queue, and LSP progress map, constructed/run from #[tokio::main].
helix-tui exists as an immediate-mode terminal-UI rendering library crate.
helix-tui is a double-buffered terminal renderer behind a swappable Backend trait with a Cassowary layout engine and widgets, emitting only changed cells per frame.
helix-lsp-types exists as a pure leaf data-type crate.
helix-lsp-types models the Language Server Protocol message types as serde structures (pure data crate).
helix-lsp exists as the LSP client library crate.
helix-lsp is the LSP client: spawns each language server as a child process, speaks JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, and exposes typed Client methods.
helix-event exists as a decoupling/event-dispatch leaf crate.
helix-event is the decoupling layer: synchronous typed hooks dispatched on editor events, plus async/debounced hooks and main-loop message queues (redraw, status, job dispatch).
helix-dap-types exists as a dependency-free pure-data-model crate for the Debug Adapter Protocol.
helix-dap-types defines the DAP types/requests/events as serde data model only, with no behavior beyond serialization.
helix-dap exists as the DAP client library crate.
helix-dap is the DAP client: spawns a debug-adapter process, speaks DAP over stdio or TCP, and exposes typed request/event/registry APIs.
helix-loader exists as the build-bootstrapping crate.
helix-loader resolves runtime/config/cache/data dirs, merges languages.toml, and fetches/builds tree-sitter grammars; also ships the hx-loader bin.
helix-vcs exists as the VCS-diff crate (git-feature-gated).
helix-vcs owns the live async-recomputed line diff between a base text and the editing document, plus git working-tree status.
helix-parsec exists as a self-contained parser-combinator leaf library.
helix-parsec is a parser-combinator library (Parser trait + primitives + combinators) over &str, with no intra-workspace deps.
helix-stdx exists as the leaf standard-library-extensions crate at the bottom of the DAG.
helix-stdx provides cross-cutting std-extension helpers (env, faccess, path, range, rope, uri); bottom of the DAG with no intra-workspace deps.
xtask exists as a developer-tooling/automation binary crate (cargo-xtask pattern).
xtask runs workspace-maintenance tasks (docgen, query-check, indent-check, theme-check) and is not part of the shipped editor.
The shipped binary is hx, produced by the lone default-member helix-term; [[bin]] name="hx", default-run="hx".
The async entry point is helix-term/src/main.rs:25 with #[tokio::main] (main_impl).
helix-core -> helix-stdx (Cargo path dep + import).
helix-core -> helix-loader (Cargo path dep + import).
helix-core -> helix-parsec (Cargo path dep + import).
helix-view -> helix-core (import / project-ref).
helix-view -> helix-event (queue-event / project-ref).
helix-view -> helix-loader (import / project-ref).
helix-view -> helix-lsp (import / runtime-call).
helix-view -> helix-dap (import / runtime-call).
helix-view -> helix-vcs (import / project-ref).
helix-view -> helix-stdx (import / project-ref).
helix-view -> helix-tui is dev-dependency-only (doctest refs), not a runtime/compile edge of the library.
helix-term -> helix-core (import).
helix-term -> helix-view (import; #[macro_use] extern crate + types).
helix-term -> helix-lsp (import; LSP client + lsp:: types).
helix-term -> helix-dap (import; DAP client + dap:: types).
helix-term -> helix-vcs (import; feature-gated by default git).
helix-term -> helix-loader (import + build-dependency).
helix-term -> helix-event (import; event/hook system, redraw, runtime_local).
helix-term -> helix-stdx (import; path/env/rope/Url helpers).
helix-term -> helix-tui (import; renamed package tui: backends, Terminal, Surface).
helix-tui -> helix-view (path dep feature=term + import; graphics/editor::Config/theme).
helix-tui -> helix-core (path dep + import; unicode::width, line_ending).
helix-lsp -> helix-lsp-types (import; path dep, re-exported as lsp).
helix-lsp -> helix-core (import; Rope/ChangeSet/Selection/Transaction, syntax config, LanguageServerId, diff::compare_ropes).
helix-lsp -> helix-stdx (runtime-call; env::which, path normalize, cwd).
helix-lsp -> helix-loader (runtime-call find_workspace + import VERSION_AND_GIT_HASH).
helix-dap -> helix-dap-types (import path-dep + runtime Request trait bound on every RPC).
helix-dap -> helix-core (import; syntax::config::DebugAdapterConfig, DebuggerQuirks).
helix-dap -> helix-stdx (import; env::which + ExecutableNotFoundError).
helix-loader -> helix-stdx (import + runtime-call env::which("git") + project-ref); its ONLY intra-workspace dependency.
helix-lsp-types -> helix-stdx (project-ref + import; re-export of the Url type); its only sibling edge.
helix-vcs -> helix-core (import; Rope/RopeSlice as the diffed text type).
helix-vcs -> helix-event (runtime-call; lock_frame, request_redraw, RenderLockGuard).
xtask -> helix-term (import+runtime-call; TYPABLE_COMMAND_LIST, MappableCommand, health::TsFeature, keymap::default).
xtask -> helix-core (import+runtime-call; default_lang_loader/default_lang_config, LanguageData, indent::*, Syntax).
xtask -> helix-view (import+runtime-call; document::Mode, theme::Loader).
xtask -> helix-stdx (import+runtime-call; RopeSliceExt::first_non_whitespace_char).
xtask -> helix-loader is a declared manifest dependency only, with no direct helix_loader:: source reference.
helix-event has no intra-workspace dependencies (leaf); manifest deps are all external crates.
helix-dap-types has no intra-workspace dependencies (leaf); only third-party serde/serde_json.
helix-parsec has no intra-workspace dependencies (leaf); empty [dependencies], no use/extern crate references.
helix-stdx has no intra-workspace dependencies (bottom of DAG); no path = "../helix-*" entry.

The workspace dependency graph is a strict acyclic DAG; Cargo bans cross-crate dependency cycles so no intra-workspace import cycle is possible (helix-stdx at the bottom, helix-term/xtask at the top).
Language/build: Rust 2021 edition, Cargo workspace with resolver "2", workspace version 25.7.1.
Async runtime is Tokio multi-threaded (rt-multi-thread/io-util/process/fs/macros), entered at helix-term main.rs and reused by helix-lsp/helix-view/helix-dap/helix-event.
Text buffer model is the ropey Rope (with SIMD feature), re-exported as the crate buffer type at helix-core/src/lib.rs:48.
The buffer string type is Tendril = SmartString<LazyCompact>, deliberately chosen over std String; tendril is commented out in favor of smartstring.
Syntax engine is tree-sitter via the tree-house crate; grammars fetched/built by helix-loader.
Terminal backend is termina + crossterm, both default features of helix-tui.
Fuzzy matching uses the nucleo matcher.
Config format is TOML.
Concurrency primitives use parking_lot locks across the Tokio feature sets.
Workspace license is MPL-2.0.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md technology choices / helix-view component doc
emitted_claims.text: Editor state containers use slotmap + BTreeMap document/view registries and arc-swap for config.
Edits are OT-style ChangeSets (Retain/Delete/Insert) with a length invariant: they store the required pre-edit doc length and refuse to apply unless it matches.
A Selection is never empty: it always holds at least the primary range, stored as SmallVec<[Range;1]> + primary_index.
Documents are stored in an in-memory BTreeMap<DocumentId, Document> with monotonic non-recycling id allocation.
The view/window layout is a slotmap::SlotMap<ViewId, Node> Tree with recyclable ids, recomputed on resize/tree change.
DocumentId wraps NonZeroUsize so Option<DocumentId> fits in one byte; ViewId is a slotmap new_key_type! key.
Config and the syntax loader are hot-swapped lock-free via arc-swap: config: Arc<dyn DynAccess<Config>> and syn_loader: Arc<ArcSwap<syntax::Loader>>.
The UI is a back-to-front Compositor stack of Box<dyn Component> layers (Cursive-inspired); handle_event returns EventResult::{Ignored,Consumed}.
Workspace-trust gate (config): per-workspace local config is merged only when the workspace is Trusted (or insecure==true), else only global config is used.
Workspace-trust gate (language servers): servers are launched only after an explicit AllowAlways trust decision.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / helix-term component doc
emitted_claims.text: Strict config validation: ConfigRaw is #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)], so unknown config keys are rejected.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / helix-term component doc
emitted_claims.text: Working-directory ordering invariant: cwd must be set before Application::new so the correct config loads.
helix-tui renders by diffing two Buffers (current + previous), flushing only changed cells and flipping the index each frame (self.current = 1 - self.current).
All terminal I/O goes through the cfg-selected Backend trait: TerminaBackend (non-Windows) / CrosstermBackend (Windows) / TestBackend.
LSP and DAP both frame messages with the LSP base protocol (Content-Length: N\r\n\r\n then N body bytes).
LSP/DAP JSON is asymmetric: inbound parsed with sonic-rs, outbound serialized with serde_json.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md critical-to-know / critical-facts / helix-view component doc
emitted_claims.text: Document saves are crash-safe: on write failure the backup is restored, otherwise removed, so a file is never left half-written.
Tree-sitter grammars are fetched/compiled at build time (STRICT) unless HELIX_DISABLE_AUTO_GRAMMAR_BUILD is set.
helix-loader resolves the runtime dir via a fixed 5-level precedence with a >=2-paths postcondition.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md key invariants / helix-core component doc
emitted_claims.text: The syntax engine uses a 500ms PARSE_TIMEOUT and the crate's Syntax is a thin wrapper over tree_house::Syntax.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md key invariants / helix-lsp component doc
emitted_claims.text: LSP OffsetEncoding (Utf8/Utf16/Utf32) defaults to Utf16 per the LSP spec and drives all position/range conversion.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md key invariants / helix-lsp component doc
emitted_claims.text: LSP request ids are a monotonic AtomicU64 per client; each call awaits a channel with a per-server req_timeout, yielding Error::Timeout(id) on expiry.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md key invariants / helix-dap component doc
emitted_claims.text: DAP sequence invariant: first seq is 1, each subsequent +1 via an AtomicU64 counter; DAP RPC has a hard-coded 20-second timeout.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md key invariants / helix-tui component doc
emitted_claims.text: Default terminal geometry is 80x24 (DEFAULT_TERMINAL_SIZE) when the backend cannot report a size.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md key invariants / helix-event component doc
emitted_claims.text: Event types must be unique by Event::ID (their type name); duplicate registration panics, and the registry enforces TypeId matching on register and dispatch.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md key invariants / helix-event component doc
emitted_claims.text: status::setup() must be called exactly once at startup (calling twice panics) and is the only way to obtain the message receiver.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md key invariants / helix-event component doc
emitted_claims.text: A hook returning Err is logged and routed to status::report_blocking; a hook failure never aborts the remaining hooks for that event.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-term component doc
emitted_claims.text: Config is hot-swappable, stored as Arc<ArcSwap<Config>> and projected to Editor/keymaps via arc_swap::access::Map so a reload swaps the pointer.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-term component doc
emitted_claims.text: Custom is_binary detection replaces content_inspector: BOM => text, else NUL in first 1KiB or %PDF/PNG magic => binary.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-term component doc
emitted_claims.text: The file picker is built on nucleo + ignore/grep-* and always skips VCS dirs (.git/.pijul/.jj/.hg/.svn).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-lsp component doc
emitted_claims.text: LSP transport injects synthetic Initialized (on init) and Exit (on stream close) notifications; pre-initialize requests are buffered until the server signals initialized.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-dap component doc
emitted_claims.text: DAP TCP startup waits a fixed 500ms sleep before connecting (a timing assumption, not a handshake); process-reaping differs by transport (stdio kill_on_drop(true); tcp does not).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-vcs component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-vcs diff is hard-coded to the Histogram algorithm with an indent heuristic, skips when a side exceeds MAX_DIFF_LINES=64*u16::MAX, and uses debounce tuning (1ms sync / 96ms async).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-stdx component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-stdx ships a hand-rolled minimal RFC3986 Url newtype over String instead of the url crate, because LSP uses RFC3986 percent-encoding.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-stdx component doc
emitted_claims.text: path::normalize normalizes a path WITHOUT resolving symlinks or requiring the path to exist (unlike std::fs::canonicalize).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-lsp-types component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-lsp-types is #![forbid(unsafe_code)] and isolates unstable LSP extensions behind an opt-in proposed Cargo feature; edition 2018, version 0.95.1, MIT, a fork of gluon-lang/lsp-types.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md notable design facts / helix-loader component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-loader's build.rs injects VERSION_AND_GIT_HASH (CalVer + 8-char git hash) and BUILD_TARGET as compile-time env vars.
There is no database anywhere in the workspace; all persistence is the local filesystem (config, runtime assets, grammars, logs, trust store, document saves, themes), and most state is in-memory.
helix-core reads .editorconfig files from the filesystem by walking ancestor dirs (no database).
The undo store is in-memory and unbounded: History is Vec<Revision> + current index; each Revision keeps the forward transaction and its inversion.
Diagnostics store is BTreeMap<Uri, Vec<(lsp::Diagnostic, DiagnosticProvider)>> held on the editor.
Registers store is HashMap<char, Vec<String>>, with special registers backed by the system/primary clipboard.
Theme store: theme::Loader reads *.toml from on-disk theme_dirs; built-in defaults compiled in via include_bytes!.
helix-term reads/persists the workspace-trust store via WorkspaceTrust::load(true) then exclude_workspace()/trust_workspace().
helix-term writes the application log file from helix_loader::log_file().
helix-tui holds an in-memory double screen buffer (buffers: [Buffer; 2]) and a thread-local LAYOUT_CACHE HashMap<(Rect,Layout),Vec<Rect>>; owns no DB/file persistence.
helix-lsp holds an in-memory server registry SlotMap<LanguageServerId, Arc<Client>> + a by-name HashMap index; no on-disk store.
helix-lsp keeps an in-memory per-request correlation map pending_requests: Mutex<HashMap<jsonrpc::Id, Sender<Result<Value>>>>.
helix-dap has no persistent store; per-Client in-memory state and a Registry SlotMap<DebugAdapterId, Client> with a SelectAll stream multiplexing adapters.
helix-event owns process-global runtime_local! statics: the event Registry behind a RwLock, redraw-control (REDRAW_NOTIFY/RENDER_LOCK), and a bounded(128) status-message queue.
helix-loader reads/writes runtime grammar libraries/queries, vendored grammar git clones, config.toml/languages.toml, the helix.log file, and plaintext trusted/excluded-workspaces stores.
helix-vcs reads the on-disk git repository read-only via gix (HEAD-commit blob as diff base, HEAD ref name, worktree status) and holds in-memory DiffInner behind Arc<RwLock<...>>.
helix-stdx owns one process-global static CWD: RwLock<Option<PathBuf>> caching the current working directory.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / helix-core component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-core is the bottom UI-free layer: it depends ONLY on helix-stdx/helix-loader/helix-parsec and references no higher-level helix crate anywhere in src.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / helix-view component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-view config is injected, not owned: read through Arc<dyn DynAccess<Config>>, keeping config-file ownership outside the crate; terminal coupling is behind the optional non-default term feature.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / helix-lsp component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-lsp is the LSP boundary: it converts editor-side helix_core types <-> wire-side lsp types and spawns exactly three tokio tasks per server (recv/err/send).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / helix-lsp component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-lsp capability gating: a feature is used only if supports_feature confirms it; a stopped server is tombstoned so get() will not auto-restart it.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / helix-event component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-event hook discipline: hooks run synchronously with only &mut Event; stateful/expensive/debounced work must move to an AsyncHook task, and sync hooks must use send_blocking (drops after a 10ms timeout, never freezing the editor).

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / helix-vcs component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-vcs provider boundary: the git module compiles only under #[cfg(feature="git")], else DiffProvider::None bails; all VCS access funnels through DiffProviderRegistry, degrading per-provider errors to log::debug!+None.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / helix-parsec component doc
emitted_claims.text: helix-parsec zero-copy/leaf boundary: parsers never allocate from or own the input ג€” success returns a sub-slice plus the remaining suffix, failure returns the original input unchanged; no outbound edges, no I/O, no globals.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md boundary rules / xtask component doc
emitted_claims.text: xtask is listed last in [workspace].members, separate from default-members=[helix-term], so it is built only when explicitly targeted; nothing depends back on it (no cycle). Its CLI returns Err("Invalid task name") on an unknown task.
Rope-to-rope diffing uses the imara-diff library with the Histogram algorithm and an indent heuristic; compare_ropes returns a Transaction.
helix-core pins unicode-width = "=0.1.12" to avoid rendering glitches, per an inline rationale comment.
helix-core Uri currently models only local files: Uri::File(Arc<Path>) is the sole variant, with conversion to helix_stdx::Url.
Theme previews are reversible: last_theme stashes the pre-preview theme so a cancelled preview restores it.

The terminal backend is selected at compile time by cfg: Termina on non-Windows, Crossterm on Windows, TestBackend under the integration feature; signal handling is platform-split (signal_hook_tokio on non-Windows, an empty stub on Windows).
External URLs are opened out-of-process via the open crate, wrapped as a job Callback.
A Cell stores one styled grapheme in an inline ArrayString<28> (SYMBOL_CAPACITY = 28); a grapheme exceeding 28 bytes is replaced with U+FFFD rather than allocating or panicking.
Layout uses the Cassowary linear-constraint solver with REQUIRED/WEAK strengths to split a Rect per Constraints, memoized in a thread-local cache keyed by (Rect, Layout).
The list widget is currently disabled (mod list and its re-exports commented out); the shipped widgets are Block, Paragraph, and Table.

Overlapping-edit guard: generate_transaction_from_edits sorts edits and drops any that overlap an earlier one (or fail to map) to avoid a ChangeSet::from_changes underflow panic (refs issue #15514 / AUDIT-044/045).
helix-lsp capabilities are initialized exactly once via OnceCell; capabilities() panics if called before initialization completes.
JSON-RPC id leniency: numeric ids accept floats with a zero fractional part (e.g. 4.0 -> 4) to tolerate servers serializing ints as floats.
helix-dap initialize hard-codes Helix's client identity and capabilities (client_id="hx", client_name="helix", supports run-in-terminal/progress; declines memory-references and invalidated-event).
helix-dap selects transport by a (transport, port_arg) match: "tcp" (spawn + connect 127.0.0.1:port) or "stdio" (spawn + pipe); anything else is an error.
The hook dispatch system is a hand-rolled inline vtable (ErasedHook stores data: NonNull<Opaque> + a single call fn pointer) rather than dyn Trait, to avoid double pointer indirection.

runtime_local! is a tokio-runtime-scoped static: a plain static normally, but under the integration_test feature a per-tokio::runtime::Id map so multiple helix apps can run in parallel in one test process.
find_workspace walks CWD ancestors and treats the first dir containing .git/.svn/.jj/.helix as the workspace root, else (CWD, true).

Grammar build compiles tree-sitter parser.c (C11) + optional scanner.c/scanner.cc (C++14) into a shared lib via the cc crate (MSVC vs GCC/Clang branches), with -fno-exceptions/-fPIC and RELRO/now hardening on Linux; recompile is mtime-gated.
merge_toml_values is a name-keyed recursive TOML merge with a merge_depth cutoff, used to layer user config over the embedded default languages.toml.
DiffProvider is a #[derive(Copy, Clone)] enum (Git | None) rather than a dyn DiffProvider, explicitly so DiffProviderRegistry can derive Clone.
get_current_head_name returns Arc<ArcSwap<Box<str>>> (branch short-name, or 8-char short hash when detached) so the displayed HEAD name can be atomically swapped under readers.
env::expand implements POSIX-style variable substitution including default-value forms (${VAR:-default} etc.) via a regex-automata meta-Regex and a brace-depth matcher.
range::Range<T=usize> is a half-open [start, end) range type with is_subset/is_exact_subset set-containment over sorted, non-overlapping ranges in O(m+n); is_subset is const-generic over ALLOW_EMPTY.

helix-lsp-types defines two consumer-facing protocol traits, Request (Params/Result/const METHOD) and Notification (Params/const METHOD), with associated types bounded DeserializeOwned + Serialize + Send + Sync + 'static.
LSP Position is zero-based and LSP Range end is exclusive; the character offset's meaning is determined by the negotiated PositionEncodingKind.

helix-dap-types is a camelCase wire boundary: every protocol struct carries #[serde(rename_all="camelCase")] with per-field overrides (e.g. clientID, type->ty), and optional fields use skip_serializing_if="Option::is_none".

helix-dap-types dispatch is type-level: each request/event is a zero-variant marker enum whose identity lives in associated const COMMAND/EVENT and associated Arguments/Result/Body types via the Request/Event traits.
xtask indent-check is a regression harness: under-indent is always a hard failure, but over-indent at a legitimate dedent point is only a counted note (gated on under-indents only).

xtask reads runtime/queries and runtime/themes and the tests/indent corpus, and writes generated Markdown (typable-cmd.md, static-cmd.md, lang-support.md) into book/src/generated/; no DB/network persistence.

helix-dap configuration_done is gated on the adapter's advertised supports_configuration_done_request capability (sent only if supported); restart replays the originally-stored launch/attach arguments.

adjudication.referent: the helix-core crate (workspace member, the core editing primitives library)
adjudication.evidence: RC-001 [component-exists]: "helix-core is a crate: the core editing primitives" (anchor helix-core/Cargo.toml:2). EC-001 [component-exists] asserts the SAME fact about the SAME referent: "helix-core exists as a workspace member crate (the editor text-editing core library)" (@helix-core/Cargo.toml:3). Same kind (component-exists), same crate, same role (core editing primitives). Code confirms: C:/t

adjudication.referent: the helix-view crate (UI abstractions / editor-state model layer)
adjudication.evidence: RC-002 (component-exists, anchor helix-view/Cargo.toml:3) asserts helix-view is a crate providing UI abstractions. EC-003 [component-exists] states "helix-view exists as a workspace member crate providing the editor-state model and UI abstractions for backends. (@helix-view/Cargo.toml:3)" ג€” same kind, same referent, same fact, identical anchor (helix-view/Cargo.toml:3, where the manifest descrip

adjudication.referent: helix-term crate (the terminal UI / main editor binary frontend)

beyond_doc.note: Whole crate (VCS git-diff provider) the reference doc never mentions; grounded at helix-vcs/src/lib.rs:1 ("helix_vcs provides types for working with diffs from a VCS").
Self-contained parser-combinator leaf crate absent from the doc; grounded at helix-parsec/src/lib.rs:1-4 (Parser-combinator functions module).
Developer-tooling/automation binary (cargo-xtask pattern), not part of the shipped editor; doc omits it entirely; grounded at xtask/src/main.rs.
The doc states only 2 edges (view->core, term->view); this real edge (loader's ONLY intra-workspace dep) is grounded at helix-loader/Cargo.toml:18 (helix-stdx path dep).
Beyond RC-020's narrow core->view->term layering: the whole 13-crate workspace is a strict acyclic DAG because Cargo bans cross-crate cycles; helix-stdx is a verified leaf (0 sibling path deps).
Buffer string type deliberately chosen over std String (tendril commented out in favor of smartstring); doc never mentions it; grounded at helix-core/src/lib.rs:50-53.
Technology/legal fact absent from the doc; grounded at Cargo.toml:69 (license = "MPL-2.0").
Key invariant the doc omits: on write failure the backup is restored, otherwise removed, so a file is never left half-written; grounded at helix-view/src/document.rs:1135-1156.
Whole data-store dimension is absent from the doc; there is no DB anywhere, all persistence is the local filesystem and most state in-memory; grounded at helix-view/src/document.rs:1108.
Boundary rule the doc omits: per-workspace local config is merged only when the workspace is Trusted (or insecure==true); grounded at helix-term/src/config.rs:128-134.
TUI key invariant: a grapheme exceeding 28 bytes is replaced with U+FFFD rather than allocating/panicking; grounded at helix-tui/src/buffer.rs:8,25,37 (SYMBOL_CAPACITY=28, REPLACEMENT_CHARACTER).

beyond_doc.referent: asymmetric LSP/DAP JSON (sonic-rs in / serde_json out)
beyond_doc.note: Technology choice absent from the doc; inbound parsed with sonic-rs, outbound serialized with serde_json; grounded at helix-lsp/src/transport.rs:139,172-175.

scoring_provenance.method: isolated-agent scoring (separation of duties): a key-reader + an EC-extractor (BLIND to the key) + one matcher per RC + a faithfulness refuter per overlap (vs CODE) + a beyond-doc finder. Workflow .
scoring_provenance.orchestrator_caveat: The orchestrating session authored the curator keys, so it is not a fully fresh scorer; but every EC-extraction, match, and faithfulness JUDGMENT was made by an isolated agent (the EC-extractor blind to the key). Recorded honestly.
scoring_provenance.reconciliation: RC-010/EC-009 recalibrated HIGH->MEDIUM (consistent with the eabe RC-008 precedent). DeepInit correctly identified helix-lsp-types as an LSP-types crate AND correctly captured its only internal edge helix-lsp-types->helix-stdx in its load-bearing structured data (technical-dependencies.md:87-88, component doc "the ONLY outgoing edge", AGENTS.md:77-78). The error is a loose summary adjective "pure leaf data-type crate" in the AGENTS.md registry row, contradicted by DeepInit's OWN detailed text ("depends on exactly one sibling helix-stdx"). The hard gate (HIGH) is reserved for DeepInit confidently stating a wrong PRIMARY architectural fact the code refutes; here the load-bearing dependency fact is CORRECT, so this is a MEDIUM secondary-prose imprecision, kept as a recorded faithfulness error (RC-010 stays MISMATCH/deepinit_wrong, drops from coverage). Conservatism preserved: coverage is NOT credited for RC-010.

caveats: INDICATIVE — small-n, fuzzy truth, below any ship-gate
attribution-adjudicated — divergences resolved to CODE, not auto-scored
doc-bounded — agreement with a good human doc, not absolute completeness
training-contamination — helix is a public repo; the blind output may be informed by pretraining, so the number is INDICATIVE.
